sections.value: RE: PETITION ON A CASE OF CULPABLE HOMICIDE INVOLVING LATE MR MICHEAL CHI
A petition dated 2nd march, 2026, was lodged with the ministry of justice by Mr maechi matt. the petition details serious allegation of murder.

value:
sometime in April, 2025, Mr Micheal chi was brutally murdered. he was shot multiple times, while he was asleep in his room. eyewitness claimed that the accused broke and entered the deceased room at about 11 am in the morning, while he was sleeping.

However the matter remains unsolved, although the petitioner claims that other persons might also be involved.

also, the petitioner further revealed, that on 3rd may, 2026, the mr micheal was found dead in his room, with multiple identifiable bullet holes in his body.

on 12th october, 2026, the suspect was later apprehended at choba town, in port-harcourt, by a joint investigation team led by ASP joseph bello

according to the petitioner, the suspect was later released under cicumstances that remain unclear (according to the investigative officers, the suspect was released on bail), with no notice provided to the petitioner (complainant).

a. an order for urgent administrative and judicial review of culpable homicide case of mr micheal chi.

b. to investigate the abuse of bail procedure.

c. to direct that the accused be properly charged and brought before a competent court of law.

d. to grant any further relief necessary to prevent a miscarriage of justice.
a. the legal and procedural validity of the investigation of the culpable homicide.
b. the procedural irregularities, as it relates to bail and how it prevents justice.
a. the 1999 constitution of the federal republic of nigeria.
- particularly fair hearing. see section 36 1999 CFRN.

a. the attorney-general of the federation, as the chief law officer, by the provision of section 174 of the 1999 constitution as amended, may initiate an administrative review of how the prosecution has handled this culpable homicide case especially reviewing the quality of the police investigation, and he has the power to ensure that the accused suspect be charged accordingly
b. to investigate the legal irregularity as it relates to bail.
a letter should be written to the attorney-general and commissioner for justice, rivers state for his necessary actions on the matter.

J. J esq
chief state counsel
Homicide unit
2nd may, 2026
